paper.title: Design of Internal Contact-induced Multiple Quasi-zero Stiffness using Capped Conical Shells
paper.abstract: Isolation (41S 절연) Low-frequency vibration degrades system durability and human health. Quasi-Zero Stiffness (QZS) mechanisms offer effective low-frequency isolation by drastically reducing dynamic natural frequency. However, conventional QZS structures suffer from load sensitivity, limiting their application outside narrow design loads. This study proposes a mass-producible capped conical shell structure that induces internal contact between two distinct nonlinear components to realize a secondary QZS region. This multiple QZS mechanism successfully extends the effective load range by over 200% compared to a single structure. Additionally, its modular design allows simple series connections to flexibly accommodate various target loads. Optimal geometric parameters were derived using a surrogate model based on a nonlinear hyperelastic Finite Element Method (FEM) database. Static compression tests on a 3D-printed TPU prototype experimentally verified the two designed QZS regions. The proposed mechanism is highly promising for vibration isolation in vehicle platforms and precision machinery subject to fluctuating payloads. — - 006 — 250 0 er pS ~ 150 ; : a 이 = 100 30 4 i 0 amie: 0 2 4 6 8 10 19 ities Displacement(mm)
